naming_inspiration.description: Middle name honors his father Cyprian Hart, who died when Henry was five years old.

institutions.institution_blurb: Yale Medical M.D. 1826; began practice in his native Kensington parish.

burial.plot_notes: Ledge Cemetery, Berlin CT. FindAGrave Memorial #62169513.

education.notes: M.D. 1826. Attended two courses of lectures per Yale Biographical Notices.

career.organization: Private practice, Kensington Parish, Berlin CT
career.notes: Started practice in his native parish immediately after receiving M.D. Died in practice March 28, 1831 age 30.

narrative_blocks.header: Raised by his widowed mother and adoptive great-aunt
narrative_blocks.body: Born January 30, 1801 at the General Hart homestead in Kensington, fifth child and younger son of Cyprian Hart and Lucy Hooker. His father died March 1, 1806 when Henry was five years and one month old. He was raised in the household of his widowed mother Lucy and her adoptive aunt Ruth Cole Hart, who together kept the homestead from 1806 onward.

narrative_blocks.header: Yale Medical degree at twenty-five, physician at twenty-six
narrative_blocks.body: He graduated from Yale College in the Class of 1822 at age twenty-one, then attended two courses of lectures at the Yale Medical School and received the M.D. degree in 1826 in his twenty-fifth year. He began practice in his native Kensington parish almost immediately.

narrative_blocks.header: A man of high promise, dead at thirty
narrative_blocks.body: He practiced medicine in Kensington for five years and died there on March 28, 1831, aged thirty, unmarried. The Yale Biographical Notices summary records him as regarded as a man of high promise. His sister Emily, also unmarried, would die at the same Kensington parish four years later at age twenty-nine.

burial.plot_notes: Ledge Cemetery, Berlin CT. FindAGrave Memorial #61935571.

cross_connections.display_label: Connecticut Militia General who took him in at four
cross_connections.notes: Cyprian's father Nathaniel Hart No. 2 died October 13, 1773 when Cyprian was four. His childless uncle Gen. Selah Hart (whose only natural child Mary had died young) adopted him as heir to the General Hart homestead. Cyprian died March 1, 1806; Selah died fourteen weeks later on June 10, 1806.

cross_connections.display_label: Two Cyprian Harts, both adopted by Gen. Selah Hart
cross_connections.notes: H00562 (this entry) and H00568 both bore the name Cyprian Hart and both were documented as adopted heirs of General Selah Hart (H00273) — a striking naming + adoption coincidence.

narrative_blocks.header: Father dead at four, raised on uncle's homestead
narrative_blocks.body: When Nathaniel Hart died at Kensington on October 13, 1773, his sixth son Cyprian was four years old. Widowed Martha Norton soon removed to Hartford, and the boy was taken in by his childless uncle General Selah Hart and raised on the General Hart homestead as adopted heir.

narrative_blocks.header: Married Lucy Hooker at twenty-three, both born 1772
narrative_blocks.body: On December 24, 1795 he married Lucy Hooker of the same Kensington parish, born four months and ten days after him in January 1772. They were second cousins through their shared great-grandfather, the Honorable John Hooker of Farmington. Six children arrived in nine years.

narrative_blocks.header: Died before the General who had raised him
narrative_blocks.body: He died at Kensington on March 1, 1806, aged thirty-seven, leaving his youngest daughter Emily nine months old. General Selah Hart followed him into the grave fourteen weeks later, on June 10, in the same house. By the adoption arrangement the General Hart estate passed through his nephew's six children rather than to Selah's collateral kin.

narrative_blocks.header: Two widows shared the house thirty-eight years
narrative_blocks.body: After both husbands died in the spring of 1806, his widow Lucy Hooker Hart and his adoptive aunt Ruth Cole Hart kept the Kensington homestead together for the rest of their lives. Ruth died in the house in January 1844 at one hundred and one. Lucy lived another seven years alone, dying there in July 1851.

cross_connections.display_label: Co-widow in the General Hart house thirty-eight years
cross_connections.notes: After Cyprian Hart died March 1, 1806 and General Selah Hart died June 10, 1806 in the same house, Lucy (then 34) and Ruth Cole Hart (then 63) — wives of nephew and adoptive uncle — kept the General Hart homestead together for as long as they lived. Ruth died there January 15, 1844 at 101 years, 2 months, 16 days. Lucy lived another seven years alone, dying July 8, 1851 at 79. EH #537: 'his widow and the widow of his uncle Gen. Selah Hart occupied the General Hart house and property together as long as they lived.'

burial.plot_notes: Ledge Cemetery, Berlin CT. FindAGrave Memorial #61935595.

narrative_blocks.header: Widowed at thirty-four with six young children
narrative_blocks.body: She lost her husband Cyprian Hart on March 1, 1806 and his adoptive uncle General Selah Hart fourteen weeks later on June 10. By the adoption arrangement she remained mistress of the General Hart homestead with her six children, the youngest only nine months old. She had all six baptized at Kensington church the following November.

narrative_blocks.header: Forty-five years a widow, three children unmarried
narrative_blocks.body: Of her six children, three died unmarried before their forty-second year: Selah at forty-one in 1838, the physician Henry Cyprian at thirty in 1831, and Emily at twenty-nine in 1835. She herself died July 8, 1851 at Kensington aged seventy-nine — having outlived her husband by forty-five years and having shared the General Hart house with her co-widow Ruth Cole Hart for thirty-eight of them.

cross_connections.display_label: Susannah Judd's family name connects to the Judd family of.

bio.notes: Widow of Samuel Seymour of Kensington before marrying Elijah Hooker

William Banfield Capron, Madura mission; Judge John Hooker, member.

Founded 1810 as the Medical Institution of Yale College, the fourth oldest medical school in the United States.
